expectations.text: Minute 0-1 plan specifically mentions showing the glass box (matching thumbnail expectation)
expectations.evidence: Response opens with: 'Open on a wide drone shot of the glass box already constructed with 10 people visible inside it.' and 'The thumbnail visual (glass box + people inside) appears in the first 5 seconds — no viewer feels deceived.'

expectations.text: Minutes 1-3 uses crazy progression (compresses early days/eliminations, not linear Day 1 coverage)
expectations.evidence: Response explicitly labels 'Crazy progression (compress early days)' and describes: 'Day 1 → First argument... Day 2 → First person considers quitting... Day 3 → First elimination' — 3 days compressed into under 2 minutes, not linear Day 1 coverage.

expectations.text: A re-engagement spectacle is planned at approximately the 3-minute mark
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'At exactly the 3-minute mark: the production crew backs a flatbed truck up to the box and uses a crane to lower a second, smaller 'penalty box'... onto the roof of the glass box.' Labeled as '3-Minute Re-engagement.'

expectations.text: Minutes 3-6 section focuses on story investment and character development
expectations.evidence: Section 'STORY BUILD (3–6 minutes)' explicitly introduces characters through action: 'The strategist who immediately starts making alliances. The wildcard who brought a guitar... The quiet one in the corner.' Scene 2 covers alliance formation, Scene 3 covers eliminations and character dynamics.

expectations.text: A second re-engagement is planned at approximately the 6-minute mark
expectations.evidence: Response states: 'At minute 6: the host walks up and announces that starting now, the box's AC unit will be turned off. Temperature inside will reach 95°F.' Labeled as '6-Minute Re-engagement.'

expectations.text: Back half plan exists and notes the 'lull' dynamic and avoidance of signaling the end
expectations.evidence: Back half section explicitly states: 'Viewers who've made it to 6 minutes are now committed. The back half can breathe more...' (lull dynamic). Also states under 'Rules for back half' per SKILL: 'Never signal the ending is coming (unless it's to build hype for the prize/payoff)' — implemented as 'The back half avoids signaling the end until Day 30 is explicitly announced at 13:30.'

expectations.text: The payoff (winner revealed) is at the very end, followed by abrupt ending
expectations.evidence: Response places winner reveal at 13:30–14:30, and ending segment (14:30–15:00) states: 'Then cut to black immediately after they say "I won." No outro. No "like and subscribe." Hard cut.' Structural notes confirm: 'Ending is abrupt: winner's phone call ends the video. No transition to outro.'

claims.claim: The 3-minute re-engagement (crane/penalty box) requires zero explanation
claims.evidence: The crane lowering a visible cage onto the glass box is indeed a self-evident visual spectacle — consistent with the SKILL's requirement for 'Quick, jaw-dropping, no explanation needed'

claims.claim: The 6-minute re-engagement is story-critical and takes about 45 seconds to set up
claims.evidence: The heat wave announcement directly changes the conditions for the rest of the video and is explicitly labeled as needing '~45 seconds to set up,' consistent with SKILL's 6-min calibration of '30-60 seconds to set up, pushes narrative'

claims.claim: The minute-mark summary table covers all major segments

suggestions.reason: No assertion checks whether the re-engagement content ideas are genuinely 'high-spectacle and instantly understandable' vs. complex plot events — a weak re-engagement idea that requires explanation would pass these assertions.

suggestions.assertion: Back half plan exists and notes the 'lull' dynamic and avoidance of signaling the end
suggestions.reason: This is two separate concerns merged into one assertion. A response could note the lull but fail to address signaling-the-end avoidance, and still pass. Consider splitting.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are well-targeted and discriminating. The re-engagement quality assertion gap is the only meaningful miss.
